California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
February 11. l-991
Mr. P. Lasrrence Klose
office of the city Attorney
P.O. Box 7540
Mountain vierr, cA 94039
1.
dec i s ion
the sites
f inanc ing
2.
bank as a
depos it?
1.
regarding
Dear Mr. Klose:
This j.s in response to your request for advice on behalf of
city council,member Dena Bonnel} regarding the conflict of interest
provisions of the Political Reforrn Act (the trActtr).r Since your
Ietter provides general information, we are treating your request
as one ior informal assistance.2
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-9 0-74 3
OUESTIONS
May city councilmember Dena Bonne]l participate in a
regarding a proposed site for a new Ij-brary when one of
under consideration belongs to a bank which provides
for the oil company that employs councilmember BonneII?
Does a public official 
have a financial interest in a
result of receiving interest from a bank account
CONCLUSTONS
councilnember Bonnell may participate in a decision
a site for a new library unless her employer and the
1 Go.r".rr."nt code Sections 81ooo-91015. A1t statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Cornmission
regulations appear at 2 california Code of Regul,ations Section
I-8OOO, et seo. AIt references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
2 rnformal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
irnmunity provided by an opinion or formaL reritten advice.
(covernment code Section 83114,' 2 cal. code of Regs.
section 18329 (c) (3). )
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 . Sacramento CA 95804-0807 . (916) 3 22-5660


Our Fi■e No. I-90-743
Page 2
bank that owns one of the proposed sites are 'rotherrrise related
business entitiesI as discussed in Regulation 18236(b).
2. 
Interest from a bank deposit is not income as defined in
Section 82030 and does not constitute a financial interest as
contemplated by Section 87103.
FACTS
City councilmember Dena BonneLL is a paid financial
consultant who receives in excess of $250 per year for her
services from an oil conpany. The oil company has an ongoing
business relationship with a very large, statewide banking
institution 
('rthe bank't) which provides an annual line of credit
to the oit conpany. The oil company maintains its business
accounts at the bank rrhich furnishes the line of credit. 
The oiI
company receives virtually aII of its financing for its operations
from the bank, which receives rnonthly reports and conducts an
annual audit relating to the financial condition of the oil
company. Councilrnernber Bonnell maintains her personal bank
accounts srith the same bank.
The City of Mountain View is considering construction of a
new downtown library. 
several sites are being considered,
including one which is the site of the bank's downtown branch.
You are questioning whether councilmernber Bonnell may
participate in the site selection decision, in light of her
employment relationship with the oil company and the interest
received from the bank fron her personal accounts.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits public officials 
frorn making,
participating in, or using their official position to influence
any governmental decision in which they know or have reason to
kno!, they have a financial interest. 
whether a public official
has a financial interest in a decision is governed by Section
87103, which provides, in part:
An official 
has a financial interest in a
decision within the neaning of section 87100 if it
is reasonably foreseeable that the decision wilI
have a material financial effect, distinguishable
from its effect on the public generally, on the
official 
or a nember of his or her immediate family
or on:
(c) Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a connercial lending
institution 
in the regular course of business on


Our Fi■e No. I-90-743
Page 3
terms avaifable to the public without regard to
official 
status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($25o) or more in value provided to,
received by or prornised to the pubLic official
within 12 months prior to the time $/hen the
decision is made.
(d) Any business
official 
is a director,
employee, or holds any
entity in which the pub■ic
officer′  partner′  trustee′
pos■tiOn of management.
section 87■03(c)′  (d)。
Cj-ty councilmernbers are public officials subject to the
conf l ict-of- interest provisions of the Act. 
(Section a2o4ai
Regulation 18700, copy enclosed. ) Therefore, councilmembers nay
not participate in or use their official position to influence any
governmental decision which witl have a reasonabfy foreseeable and
material effect on their economic interests, including any sources
of incorne to them.
Councifmember Bonnell received more than S25O per year from
her work for the oil company, making the oil company a source of
income. Therefore, she must disqualify herself fron any decision
which will foreseeably and rnaterially affect the oil cornpany where
such effect is distinguishable from the effect on the public
general ly .
The effects of a decision are reasonably foreseeable if there
is a substantial liketihood that they will occur. To be
foreseeable, the effects of a decision must be more than a mere
possibility; 
however, certainty is not required. 
(Downev Cares v.
Downev Communitv Devel,opment com. (1987) l-96 Cal .App.3d 983, 898-
991; Witt v. Morrow (1977) 70 ca].App.3d 817, A22i fn re: Thorner
(1975) 1 FPPC ops. 198 (copy enclosed). ) The Act seeks to prevent
more than actual conflicts of interest; it seeks to prevent even
the appearance of a possible conflict of interest' 
(witt v.
Morrow, .Sllp.IE r at 823. )
Fron the information provided, it does not appear reasonably
foreseeabfe that the sale by the bank of one of its branch sites
will have a material financial effect upon one of many cornpanies
to which the bank furnishes a line of credit. 
It is extrenely
unlikely that the divestiture by the bank, a large statewide
institution, 
of one of its nunerous branches $/ould have any effect
at alt upon the oiI cornpany, unless the bank and the oi1 company
that enploys Ms. Bonnelt are otherwise reLated business entities,
as discussed in Regulations 18706 and 18236 (copies enclosed).
Regulation 18706 provides that an official 
has a financial
interest in a decision "if it is reasonably foreseeable that the
decision will have a naterial financial effect, distinguishable
from its effect on the pubJ,ic generally, on a business entity


Our File No. I-90-743
Page 4
lrhich is a parent or subsidiary of I or is otherwise related to a
business entity in which the official 
has one of the interests
defined in Government code Section 87103 (a), (c), or (d).rl
Sj,nce council,roember BonneII receives more than $25o per year
from the oil company, her financial interest in the company fa11s
within Section 87103(c) and (d), quoted above.
Regulation 18235(b) states that business entities whj-ch are
not in i parent-subsidiary relationshj-p are otherwise related if
any one of the folJ.owing three tests is met:
(1) one business entitY has a
controlling ownership interest in the other
business entity.
(2) There is shared rnanagernent and
control betl^reen the entities. 
In deterrnining
lrhether there is shared management and
control, consideration should be given to the
following factors:
(A) The same person or
substantj.ally the same person owns and
manages the two entities;
(B) There are common or commingled
funds or assets I
(c) The business entities share the
use of the same offices or employees, or
otherwise share activities, 
resources or
personnel on a regular basis;
(D) There is otherwise a regular
and close l^rorking relationship between
the entities; or
(3) A controllin!, owner (50* or greater
interest as a shareholder or as a general
partner) in one entity also is a controlling
owner i-n the other entitY.
You have indicated to me in telephone conversations that to
your knosrledge, the conditions set forth in ReguJ.ation 18235 are
not met by the bank and the oil company, except that the bank does
require nonthly reports and annual audits in connection with its
agieernent with the oiI company to extend a line of credit. 
You
hid sone concern that the reporting and audit requirenents would
bring the two entities into a regular and close working
relaiionship as set forth in Regulation 18236(b) (2) (D). It should
be borne in nind that the regular and close relationship cited in
Regulation 18236(b) (2)(D) is one of four listed factors and that
such a relationship would not automatically trigger a


Our Fi■e No. I-90-743
Page 5
determination that the bank and the oil company were othervrise
related business entities.
Given the size of the bank and the structured reportj-ngt
required of the oil company, there appears to be herein a
customary arrangement by a Lender to monitor the financial
condition of an ongoing debtor rather than a I'reguLar and close
working relationship.tt 
The regulation cited above looks for a
substantial operatj-ona1 connection, (see Lvders advice letter,
No. A-85-117, copy enclosed. ) Consequently, under the facts you
have provided, it does not appear that the bank and the oil
conpany are otherwise related business entiti,es. 
Absent such a
relationshj,p, there v,routd be no basis for councilmember Bonnell to
disqualify herself from participating in the Iibrary site
selection decision.
of course, if there are facts indicating that the factors set
forth in Regulation f.8236(b) are present, then there could be a
basis for finding that the two entities are otherwise related
business entities. 
In the event that a determination is made that
the bank and oi1 company are refated business entj-ties, the
pivotal, issue would be r.rhether the site sel-ection decision would
have a foreseeable rnaterial f inanci-aI effect on the bank.
However, the facts you have furnished do not seem to show that the
entities are closely related as contemplated bv Regulation 18236,
and it appears that the councilmember may participate in the site
selection decision.
As to your question regarding a possible conflict of interest
resulting from the interest received by the councilrnember from the
bank, be advised that Section 82030 specifically excludes interest
from a time or demand deposit in a financial institution 
frorn the
definition of income in the Act. Thus, the bank is not a source
of income to councilmernber Bonne]I.
I trust the above answers your questions. If you need
further j.nforrnation, please do not hesitate to call ne at (916)
322-5901,.
Sincerely′
SCOTT HALLABRIN
Acting Ceneral Counsel
Counsel′  Legal Division
SH:SB:KEN
Enc losures
